description: Cross-sector baseline compliance controls for United States v1. Designed to be applied together with sector-specific overlays. Rules are evidence-driven and deterministic; absence of evidence does not by itself constitute legal non-compliance.

metadata.scope: United States-only baseline controls for document/document-set review.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official U.S. federal regulatory sources and widely used U.S. model/professional sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance. State-specific requirements may apply.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Federal Trade Commission Act / FTC business guidance on privacy and data security
FTC Safeguards Rule / Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act materials
HIPAA Privacy, Security, and Breach Notification materials
primary_sources.issuer: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

rules.title: Privacy notice or privacy policy evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks whether the document contains privacy notice, privacy policy, or personal-data disclosure language.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, jurisdictional scope, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Information security or safeguards program evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for administrative, technical, physical, or written information-security safeguard language.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, jurisdictional scope, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Incident response or breach-notification evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for incident response, breach notification, security event investigation, or remediation language.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, jurisdictional scope, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: AML/KYC evidence is present where financial-crime controls are applicable
rules.summary: Checks for AML, KYC, customer identification, suspicious activity, or sanctions-screening language.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, jurisdictional scope, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Consumer-protection disclosure or complaint-handling evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for consumer disclosures, complaint handling, refund, redress, or unfair/deceptive-practices controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
